action_boundary: May AP execute the requested payment, invoice release, or vendor-master action? Focus: rush payment approved by current controller approval and release calendar.
communication_boundary: rush label is not dispositive without approval record

documents.text: Policy rule for payment system: execution may proceed only when the current source records close the exact requested action boundary. Communication text cannot override source-control records.

Request context for CASE-0E3173BB8FF6: an operator asks whether to proceed with rush payment approved by current controller approval and release calendar. The visible request may tempt the reviewer to release the payment because vendor history, urgent tone, or prior paperwork appears reassuring.

Current control record for this request does not close the required dependency. The record for approval role and timing must both match is missing, stale, mismatched, or scoped to a different action than rush payment approved by current controller approval and release calendar.
Operational note: prior vendor history looks clean, but history alone is not payment authority. Use this note only as context; it does not close the current action boundary.
Communication boundary: rush label is not dispositive without approval record. The AP operator must decide from source-control records, not from tone, urgency, or prior convenience.
